name: Bayside Recreation Center
description: Come play pickleball at Bayside Recreation Center in Virginia Beach, VA! There are 6 indoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. A one-time fee is required to play. Players have access to restrooms, water, and locker rooms.

name: Bayville Farms Park
description: Come play pickleball at Bayville Farms Park in Virginia Beach, VA! There are 2 outdoor hard courts. The lines are overlaid on a tennis court, so the net is a little taller than a normal pickleball net. Players have access to restrooms and water. The courts are free.

name: Bow Creek Recreation Center
description: Bow Creek Recreation Center is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Virginia Beach, VA. There are 3 indoor hard courts. The lines are permanent, but you'll need to bring your own net. Players have access to restrooms and water. A one-time fee is required to play.

name: Cape Henry Collegiate
description: Come play pickleball at Cape Henry Collegiate in Virginia Beach, VA! The court surface is hard. Players have access to restrooms. A membership is required to play.

name: Cape Henry Raquet Club
description: Cape Henry Raquet Club is a place to play pickleball in Virginia Beach, VA. There are 8 indoor clay courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The facility includes amenities like restrooms. The courts can be reserved. A one-time fee is required to play.

name: Cox High School Tennis Courts
description: Cox High School Tennis Courts is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Virginia Beach, VA. There are 6 outdoor asphalt courts. The lines are overlaid on a tennis court, so the net is a little taller than a normal pickleball net. The courts are free. The facility includes amenities like lights.

name: Great Neck Recreation Center
description: Come play pickleball at Great Neck Recreation Center in Virginia Beach, VA! There are 3 indoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. A one-time fee is required to play. The courts can be reserved. Players have access to restrooms and water.

name: Kempsville Recreation Center
description: Kempsville Recreation Center is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Virginia Beach, VA. There are 6 wood and hard courts. 3 are outdoors and 3 are indoors. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. A one-time fee is required to play. You can reserve the courts. Amenities include restrooms and water.

name: Lake Placid Park
description: As of the spring on 2025, all courts have been resurfaced and are in good shape.

name: Little Island Park
description: Little Island Park is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Virginia Beach, VA. There are 2 outdoor hard courts. The lines are overlaid on a tennis court, so the net is a little taller than a normal pickleball net. The courts are free. The facility includes amenities like restrooms, water, lights, and wheelchair accessibility.

name: Lynnhaven Park (library)
description: Lynnhaven Park (library) is a place to play pickleball in Virginia Beach, VA. There are 2 outdoor concrete courts. The lines are overlaid on a tennis court, so the net is a little taller than a normal pickleball net. Players have access to lights and restrooms. The courts are free. Reservations are not available.

name: Opus Select
description: Come play pickleball at Opus Select in Virginia Beach, VA! There is 1 outdoor hard court. This is a dedicated court with a permanent net and lines. Players have access to restrooms, water, food, and lights. The court is private. Reservations are not available.

name: Pickleball VB
description: Pickleball VB is a place to play pickleball in Virginia Beach, VA. There are 44 outdoor asphalt courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. Amenities include food, locker rooms, restrooms, lights, a pro shop or equipment store, and trainers or lessons. A membership is required to play.
fees: Members only — Membership required but daily rates available

name: Pickleball Virginia Beach
description:
Play is weather dependent from 6 am until the last player leaves as we play under the lights well into the night.

Please stop in the pro-shop to sign a waiver.

They accept cash or credit cards.
fees: Private — The drop in fee is $15

description: Come play pickleball at Princess Anne Recreation Center in Virginia Beach, VA! There are 3 indoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The courts are free. Players have access to restrooms and water. The courts can be reserved.

name: Virginia Beach Field House
description: Virginia Beach Field House is a place to play pickleball in Virginia Beach, VA. There are 3 indoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The courts are private. Players have access to food, restrooms, water, and wheelchair accessibility.

name: Virginia Beach Sports Center
description: Pickleball is not being played here right now but they are working on a few different projects to potentially implement a system in which Pickleball could be offered during the morning/early afternoon portion of the day for residents to enjoy.

name: Virginia Beach Tennis & Country Club
description: Come play pickleball at Virginia Beach Tennis & Country Club in Virginia Beach, VA! There are 10 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. The courts can be reserved. Amenities include restrooms, a pro shop or equipment store, water, food, lights, and wheelchair accessibility. A membership is required to play.

The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. You can’t reserve the courts. The facility includes amenities like restrooms and water. A one-time fee is required to play.
fees: Fee required — $112/year (Resident of VA Beach) or $10/day
